footnotes.text: Only the Son can impart true knowledge concerning God the Father (John 1, 18). Those who pretend to have received further revelations, derived from sources other than Christ, are false teachers.

St. Paul adduces three points against the false teachings: (1) Christ is superior to the angels for in Him the divine nature is incarnate: through Him, not through angels, we may become "partakers of the divine nature" (2 Pet. 1, 4). (2) Our sins are forgiven, not through the agency of angels, but in our receiving at Baptism a spiritual circumcision. (3) Through His death on the cross Christ brought to an end the rule of Satan and his hosts over the world.
